learning_outcomes.description: apply selected methods of formal analysis to tonal repertoire of Western art music
engage in sensitive and nuanced discussion of tonal works from the Western art-music repertoire which shows analytical and critical awareness
read analytical and critical discussions of tonal repertoire of Western art music with clarity and confidence
understand, based on acquisition of analytical and critical skills, how tonal works of the Western art-music tradition relate as cultural objects in their time and place and in ours
communicate analytical and/or critical ideas in relation to tonal music of the Western art-music tradition in the appropriate mode, level and style

assessments.due_date:
Task 1 5/08/2026 4:50 pm

Task 2 19/08/2026 4:50 pm

Task 3 26/08/2026 4:50 pm

Task 4 2/09/2026 4:50 pm

Task 5 9/09/2026 4:50 pm

Tasks are distributed, completed and collected at the end of classes week 2 to week 7.
assessments.task_description:
Student complete regular analytical tasks in class which are collected at completion of the class.

Each of these tasks is given equal weight overall

To sit the task, students need to be physically present in class.

For students with a UQ timetable clash only, an alternative time will be provided to do this work allowing enough time for them to review the class recording.

For students who miss a task other than as a result of a regular timetable clash, they will need to provide evidence to document the reason for their absence in order to qualify for an alternative time.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Work will be collected at completion in class.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Student complete an analysis of a movement or part of a movement of instrumental music in the classical style. Analyses takes the form of an annotated score and critical observations in written form (max. 1000 words). The analysis task includes all relevant approaches studied in the course to week 9.

Further details are provided in advance of the assessment.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 1 grade for each 24 hour period from time submission is due will apply for up to 7 days. After 7 days you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Critical Analytical Discussion of Set Work
assessments.task_description:
A critical/analytical essay on a set work (to be chosen from a list) or analysis (from a set list). This is the final assessment for the course. Students are expected to draw on a range of approaches and ideas developed over the course. Word length: 2000 words plus musical examples as necessary. As a university essay, standard expectations concerning documentation of sources and referencing apply.


More details will be provided by week 11, including assessment criteria and rubrics.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The work will be submitted via Turnitin
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 1 grade for each 24 hour period from time submission is due will apply for up to 7 days. After 7 days you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.
